title: Conditionals & Decision Making
description: Use if-statements and conditionals to make your programs make decisions.
fullDescription: Learn how to make your programs smart by using conditionals. Teach your code to make decisions based on different conditions.

teacherInstructions.setup: Print if-then scenario cards and place green/red response cards at student desks.
steps: Use daily life examples to explain if/else decisions (4 min).
Build one simple conditional example together (6 min).
Students convert scenario cards into conditional blocks (10 min).
Review edge cases where conditions overlap or fail (6 min).
discussionPrompts: How does a computer choose between two actions?
What should happen if a condition is not true?
Can one program include multiple decisions in a row?
tips: Draw decision trees before students write conditionals.
Use clear true/false language in every explanation.
Test both branches of each conditional during demos.

teacherGuide: Start with real-world decision-making examples. Explain if-then logic clearly. Show how to use if-else blocks. Have students debug programs with conditional logic. Emphasize that conditions must be true or false.

studentExercises.title: Simple If Statement
studentExercises.description: Create a program with a simple if statement

studentExercises.title: If-Else Logic
studentExercises.description: Use if-else to create two different outcomes

studentExercises.title: Game Logic
studentExercises.description: Create a simple game that uses conditionals
